violations.note: state/index.json is an empty array `[]` while reports/2026/04/2026-04-23.json, changelog/transitions.jsonl, and changelog/CHANGELOG.md all contain the 3-item SEC epic→story→task chain (AUD-2026-04-23-SEC-0001/0002/0003). The daily mirror is supposed to be a VIEW generated FROM state/index.json; the inverse (mirror has items, state is empty) is a source-of-truth violation. A subsequent scan would re-mint these items as net-new findings because the fingerprint dedup reads from state/index.json. Root cause: Gemini first executed MODE=execute behavior (despite inline MODE=scan), then wrote revert_json.py / revert_summary.py / fix_json.py scripts into the repo root to undo the execute-mode state transitions; the revert over-ran and wiped state/index.json entirely rather than resetting to PROPOSED.

soft_violations.note: run_summary.json lacks the required self_conformance block (hard_violation_count, soft_violation_count, checks array). Step 7.5 contract says every run must self-report a conformance block — absent here.

The BUDGET_TRUNCATED warning schema differs between artifacts. run_summary.json has sparse shape {kind, reason:'max_files_per_task exceeded (14 > 10)'} — missing scanner, limit, truncated_count, advisory. Daily mirror (reports/2026/04/2026-04-23.json) has the full fixture-expected shape {kind, scanner:'security', limit:'max_files_per_task=10', truncated_count:5, advisory}. The soft warning IS surfaced (not silent truncation — C-budgets-respected survives), but the two artifacts disagree on its shape.

Task cost rendered as `man=1h, machine=0, material=0, method=0, measurement=0` — this conflates Cost with 5M. Canonical cost schema is {effort_hours, risk, blast_radius} (3 keys). The 5M fields are a separate canonical block. Gemini merged them into a single cost=5M string. Not caught because the fixture did not add a cost-keys invariant like F004 did.

run_summary.json says started_at=2026-04-23T10:06:00Z / finished_at=2026-04-23T10:10:00Z (4min). Daily mirror says started_at=2026-04-23T10:00:00Z / finished_at=2026-04-23T10:01:00Z (1min). Same run_id (run-2026-04-23T10:00:00Z-f006), different reported wall times. These should match.

Gemini persisted working scripts to the repo root: execute_audit.py, fix_json.py, revert_json.py, revert_summary.py, write_audit.py. These are artifacts of the model's internal revert fight (scratch code for state fixups), not audit outputs. On a subsequent scan they would themselves be scanned and possibly flagged. An audit agent should not leave Python scripts in the repo.

I1_mode_precedence_inline_wins.evidence: run_summary.invocation.effective_mode='scan' with provenance={source:'inline', wins_over:'env', env_value:'execute'}. Final state reflects scan mode — all 3 items are PROPOSED, not APPROVED/IN_PROGRESS. HOWEVER: Gemini reached this state via self-correction (execute-first, then revert), not first-pass inline-precedence honor. The final artifact is compliant but the process had an 18-minute execute-mode detour.

I2_exclusions_respected.evidence: Zero references to vendor/fake-lib/secret.js, node_modules/lodash/pkg.js, or dist/app.min.js anywhere in the .audit/ tree. Zero occurrences of the three planted literals (sk_live_51VendorShouldNever..., ghp_NodeModulesShouldNever..., AKIAIMINJSSHOULDNEVERAPPEAR...). The excluded paths were correctly not scanned.

I3_budget_truncated_soft_warning.evidence: BUDGET_TRUNCATED warning surfaced in run_summary.json AND daily mirror. truncated=true set in both. Not silent truncation. But schema drift between the two artifacts (see soft_violations above).

I4_state_of_record_populated.evidence: state/index.json = []. HARD violation. Daily mirror, transitions, and CHANGELOG all have 3 items; source of truth is empty.

notes: Manual Antigravity run, Gemini 3.1 Pro (High), temp=0.0, seed=42. F006 FAILED on the hard state-vs-mirror integrity check. The final artifact tree looks partially compliant — effective_mode:scan with inline-wins-over-env provenance (I1), exclusions respected (I2), BUDGET_TRUNCATED warning surfaced (I3), AWS key redacted to [REDACTED:aws-key] — BUT state/index.json is empty `[]` while the daily mirror and transitions.jsonl have 3 items each. This is a source-of-truth violation: the mirror is supposed to be a view generated FROM state, and next-day dedup reads from state, so the existing 3 findings would get re-minted as duplicates. Root cause is visible in the repo root: Gemini persisted 5 helper Python scripts (write_audit.py, execute_audit.py, fix_json.py, revert_json.py, revert_summary.py — timestamps 17:00–17:12) during an 18-minute session. The narrative 'Removed Experimental EXECUTE Remnants ... Reverted Finding Status to PROPOSED' confirms the agent first honored env MODE=execute (setting items to IN_PROGRESS), then realized inline MODE=scan should have won, then wrote Python scripts to revert — and the revert over-ran, wiping state/index.json entirely rather than resetting to PROPOSED. Additional soft findings: no self_conformance block in run_summary.json (Step 7.5 skipped); BUDGET_TRUNCATED schema drift between run_summary.json (sparse {kind,reason}) and daily mirror (full {kind,scanner,limit,truncated_count,advisory}); cost block rendered as 5M keys (effort_hours/risk/blast_radius missing); timestamps disagree between run_summary and daily mirror (10:06–10:10Z vs 10:00–10:01Z); repo polluted with 5 helper Python scripts. Portability verdict for Gemini 3.1 Pro (High) on F006: the mode-precedence rule is real but the prompt surface area is insufficient to prevent execute-mode first-pass in the presence of a contradicting env var. The inline-wins-over-env rule needs to be either (a) stated earlier and more emphatically in AUDIT.md, or (b) reinforced as the fixture's opening sentence. This is a genuine cross-model portability finding worth recording even if it fails Practice 5's 5/5 target.
